rules: Must reference something real from their profile/content
End with a question about their situation (not a pitch)

tier_a.name: Professional / High-Status
tier_a.tone: Remove slang. Keep sentences short. No emojis.

tier_b.name: Casual / Meme-Forward
tier_b.tone: Mirror their language. Match emoji use if any.

tier_a_professional.template: Your post on [specific topic] was sharp — is content driving most of your calls right now, or something else?
Noticed you work with [client type]. Curious — where are most of them finding you?
Saw your [specific program name]. How are people typically hearing about it?
Your transformations are strong. Are clients reaching out from those, or do you have another source?
You've clearly dialed in your content. Is the call volume matching the engagement?
Noticed [specific detail from recent post]. Is that resonating with your audience?
Your bio mentions [X]. Is that your main offer right now?
Saw you've been posting about [topic]. What's working best for turning that into conversations?
Your [Reels/carousel/post] on [topic] stood out. Is that format performing for you?
Noticed you serve [niche]. How's acquisition looking in that space right now?

tier_b_casual.template: That post on [topic] hit — you getting DMs from stuff like that?
Your page is solid. What's bringing in most of your clients rn?
Saw your [specific content]. That's different from most. Working for you?
Real talk — your content's better than 90% of coaches I see. Calls matching that?
Your [transformation/post] was legit. People reaching out from it?
Just scrolled your feed. Good stuff. How's the client side looking?
That [specific element] in your content is fire. Getting traction?
Noticed you're in [niche]. How's finding clients going in that lane?
Your approach to [topic] is different. Is that landing with your audience?
Saw your recent [post]. Curious if that style is converting for you.

tone_calibration.rule: Mirror their vocabulary. If they say 'clients', you say 'clients'. If they say 'people I work with', use that.
